events.title: Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country
events.description: Rain could alleviate conditions in mid-Atlantic and north-east, with World Cup final expected to go ahead on Sunday Warnings of dangerous conditions are expected to remain in place on Saturday across swathes of the US, amid uncertainty about where the heavy wildfire smoke swirling from the Canadian province of Ontario and the US state of Minnesota will head next. Some parts of the US mid-Atlantic and north-east regions will continue to endure poor air quality until Saturday afternoon, where there is a high chance of thunderstorms , which could bring some reprieve from the poor air but come with other risks like flash flooding and high winds. Meanwhile, parts of the midwest and Great Lakes regions will continue to see dangerous air quality. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country

events.title: Flash flood warnings strike north-east US amid wildfire smoke from Canada
events.description: Poor air quality from wildfires continues to plague New York City as several neighborhoods hit by thunderstorms Saturday is delivering a double dose of dangerous weather across the north-eastern US, with smoky skies from Canadian wildfires giving way to severe thunderstorms that have already triggered a flash flood warning as torrential rain pounds parts of the region. After a brief break on Friday, smoke from the wildfires moved back into New York City and surrounding communities on Saturday, sending air quality back into unhealthy levels . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Flash flood warnings strike north-east US amid wildfire smoke from Canada

events.title: Khamenei threatens ‘unforgettable lessons’, two US troops killed in Iranian attack
events.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Majotaba Khamenei on Saturday vowed to teach the US "unforgettable lessons" in a written message broadcast on state TV. US Central Command reported its first troop deaths due to Iranian fire when two service members were killed and an another missing in an attack on a base in Jordan.

events.title: Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike

events.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
events.description: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

events.title: Conservatives around world at war against ‘leftwing globalists’, Bridget McKenzie tells CPAC London event
events.description: Nationals senator echoes comments by Pauline Hanson as Labor criticises One Nation leader’s divisive stance Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The National party’s Senate leader, Bridget McKenzie, has used an appearance at a conference of the populist right in London to declare that conservatives are “in a war” against mass immigration, “leftwing globalists” and “woke” institutions, and to urge rightwing movements across the English-speaking world to unite and fight back. McKenzie was speaking at the inaugural CPAC GB in London, a British spinoff of the influential US Conservative Political Action Conference , organised by the former British prime minister Liz Truss. She gave a speech on Saturday morning before appearing on a panel chaired by Truss, alongside other rightwing figures. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Conservatives around world at war against ‘leftwing globalists’, Bridget McKenzie tells CPAC London event

events.title: Gulf council blasts "war crimes" by Iran
events.description: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait. The Gulf Cooperation Council has condemned Tehran's attacks on civilian sites, describing them as war crimes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf council blasts "war crimes" by Iran

events.title: Power, desalination plants hit in Kuwait
events.description: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait. The Gulf Cooperation Council has condemned Tehran's attacks on civilian sites, describing them as war crimes. Clemence Waller has the latest.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
events.description: On Wednesday, the Security Council heard an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing was held as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur

events.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
events.description: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organization before Uganda can officially be declared Ebola-free, provided no new infections are detected. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuba edges toward breakdown as power cuts and US meddling push society to brink
events.description: As Cuba swelters under six-month oil blockade imposed by US, tempers are fraying and unrest is growing When Cuba’s national grid collapses, as it did for the third time in 10 days on Tuesday, a collective groan spreads across its cities and people wonder, again, whether the island’s antiquated electricity system may soon become unrecoverable. The 777-mile Caribbean island of 9.5 million people has been sweltering under a six-month-long oil blockade imposed by the US, part of a pressure campaign to bring down its communist government. But the parlous state of Cuba’s infrastructure goes far further back. Continue reading...

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
events.description: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers with large balls of hail. Continue reading...

The Three Lions' attacking prowess proved decisive as they withstood a remarkable French second-half fightback before reasserting their dominance in stoppage time.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World Cup 2026: France escape humiliation but fall to England in Deschamps' final match

events.title: World Cup 2026 : Deschamps last dance
events.description: The match no one wants to play kicks off on Saturday in Miami. However, it is not because there is no trophy up for grabs that there is nothing to play for, England are hoping to get their hands on their best result in 60 years, whilst France looks to give their longtime manager a good send off. Kylian Mbappé will also be hoping to increase his goal tally and cement his claim to the golden boot award. Meanwhile, the countdown to the world cup final between Argentina and Spain continues, as New York still struggles with forest fire smoke from Canada.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World Cup 2026 : Deschamps last dance

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife wasn’t a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: Saka hat-trick helps England down France to win World Cup 2026 Bronze Final
events.description: Kylian Mbappe scores twice for France to hit World Cup record, but England win 6-4 in third-place playoff final.

events.title: Kylian Mbappe breaks all-time World Cup scoring record with 22nd goal
events.description: Mbappe is back on track to defend his Golden Boot title, which he and Messi have traded between themselves throughout the tournament.

events.title: World Cup final tickets near $2.3m mark on FIFA’s resale platform
events.description: While no tickets are available on FIFA's sales platform, they are being resold for sky-high prices on resale sites.

events.title: Latin America unites to cheer against Argentina in FIFA World Cup final
events.description: Fans from Mexico to Brazil unite to cheer Yamal's Spain against Argentina and Messi, whom they call 'FIFA’s golden boy'.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
events.description: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

events.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
events.description: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.

events.title: The U.S.-Iran battle over the Strait of Hormuz raises risks for global waterways
events.description: Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.

events.title: France's Mbappe sets World Cup scoring record with 22 goals
events.description: Kylian Mbappe has moved past Lionel Messi on the all-time World Cup goal-scoring record in a spectacular match for third place. England won the game 6-4.

events.title: Hungary's president signs amendment ending his own term
events.description: An ally of former PM Viktor Orban has signed a constitutional amendment ending his own term as head of state. Tamas Sulyok criticized the law, while lawmakers insist he has impeded national progress by staying in office.

events.title: World Cup 2026: France's Mbappé becomes all-time tournament goalscorer
events.description: Kylian Mbappé made history at the World Cup by becoming the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer with 22 goals after his brace in France's third-place playoff against England. The French superstar surpassed Lionel Messi's previous record of 21 goals, cementing his legacy as one of football's greatest tournament performers.

events.title: World Cup 2026: France escape humiliation but fall to England in Deschamps' final match
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Josh Kerr smashes men’s mile world record that stood unbroken since 1999
events.description: Britain's Josh Kerr clocked 3min 42.66sec in the men's mile at a high-octane Diamond League on Saturday, smashing the record set by Morocco's Hicham El Guerrouj in Rome in 1999.

events.title: Josh Kerr breaks 27-year-old mile record in front of packed London stadium
events.description: British runner Josh Kerr has broken the 27-year-old world record for the mile in front of a sell-out crowd in London.

events.title: Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'
events.description: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'

events.title: FIFA to hand World Cup winners US-style championship rings
events.description: The winners of Sunday's World Cup final between Argentina and Spain will receive a championship ring for the first time, FIFA said.

events.title: Mexico's president to attend World Cup final at Trump's invitation
events.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said late Friday she will attend Sunday’s World Cup final after an invitation from her U.S. counterpart, Donald Trump

events.title: Mount Olympus, home of ancient Greek gods, is a candidate for the World Heritage List
events.description: Mount Olympus, the mythological home of ancient Greece’s 12 Olympian gods, may soon join UNESCO’s World Heritage List

events.title: Women amputees in South America train to be World Cup soccer contenders
events.description: Women amputees from several South American countries are training in Bolivia for the 2027 Women's Amputee Football World Cup

events.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
events.description: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid

events.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
events.description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy

events.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
events.description: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says

events.title: One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent
events.description: While Boris Nadezhdin is barred from running for parliament, blogger Ilya Remeslo is remanded in custody.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent

events.title: Palestinian teenage footballer dies after Israeli settler attack
events.description: Palestinian teenage footballer, Fadi al-Naasan, has died a week after being shot by Israeli forces.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Palestinian teenage footballer dies after Israeli settler attack

events.title: A giant cake for Venezuelan quake child survivors as death toll passes 5000
events.description: Volunteers made a giant cake to cheer up around 3,000 Venezuelan children displaced by last month's powerful twin quakes
events.ticker_label: 🚑 A giant cake for Venezuelan quake child survivors as death toll passes 5000

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
events.description: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater, during a typhoon. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake

events.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
events.description: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?

events.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
events.description: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong

events.title: Pakistani forces kill 24 militants in border raids near Afghanistan
events.description: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants over the previous 24 hours. The operations were in response to attacks by militants earlier in the week.

events.title: Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition
events.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by the US Marshals Service in Miami on a sealed warrant. The UK has requested their extradition on charges that include trafficking and rape.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition

events.title: Japan's superstitions make some homes hard to sell or rent
events.description: Many Japanese people are reluctant to buy or rent homes where a sudden or violent death has occurred. To reassure prospective buyers and tenants, property owners are calling in "ghost investigators."

events.title: What's at stake for the EU's carbon market blueprint
events.description: The European Commission has confirmed it will allow EU industries to continue emitting planet-warming CO₂ gases well into the 2040s.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What's at stake for the EU's carbon market blueprint

events.title: Pogacar wins Tour de France 14th stage to extend overall lead
events.description: Slovenia's cycling phenomenon Tadej Pogacar launched a devastating late attack Saturday to win the mountainous 14th stage of the Tour de France, extending his lead over Jonas Vingegaard.

events.title: Massive fire destroys 100 homes in Norway and forces hundreds to evacuate
events.description: A major fire in southern Norway destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate the area

events.title: Peacekeepers step up support as families return to south Lebanon
events.description: With violence in south Lebanon significantly decreased since mid-June, UN peacekeepers there have returned to core functions such as protecting humanitarian aid and restoring critical infrastructure as families continue to return home.

events.title: Colombia: Pockets of insecurity challenge ‘vibrant democracy’, Security Council told
events.description: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

events.title: Boston police investigate death at home owned by Rep. Ayanna Pressley's husband
events.description: Boston police homicide unit is investigating a death at a Mattapan residence owned by Conan Harris, husband of Rep. Ayanna Pressley, D-Mass.

events.title: US Forest Service workers kidnapped, zip-tied for hours by father-son duo: California AG
events.description: A 15-hour standoff in the remote wilderness of Northern California ended peacefully early Friday morning as the FBI Hostage Rescue Team secured the release of two forest employees.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US Forest Service workers kidnapped, zip-tied for hours by father-son duo: California AG

events.title: OnlyFans model seeks dead boyfriend’s animal-cruelty warrant in self-defense murder trial: Docs

events.title: Texas dentist accused of fatal mistakes after 4-year-old dies during dental procedure
events.description: A Texas dentist allegedly failed to recognize opioid distress in a 4-year-old girl and administered the wrong reversal drug during a routine procedure.

events.title: Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift
events.description: Andy Burnham condemned Margaret Thatcher's legacy and promised public ownership of utilities, housing and transport in his first Labour leader speech.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift

events.title: Somalia races to save Radio Mogadishu’s fading archive
events.description: A small team is working to digitise decades of Somali history in the country's main public radio station.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel
events.description: Move dealt ‘severe blow to Chinese companies’ confidence in investing in the UK’, says Ministry of Commerce China’s government has said it is “strongly dissatisfied” with the decision to nationalise British Steel this week, 15 months after the UK government intervened to prevent the closure of its steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. On Thursday, British Steel was brought under public ownership to protect “the future of steel production”, the government announced. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel

events.title: Man dies after cross-border police pursuit ends in NSW northern rivers
events.description: Car chase began in Queensland after report of armed man on Gold Coast late on Saturday, police say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast New South Wales police have declared a “critical incident” after a man died following a police pursuit in the northern rivers area, hours after the same man allegedly evaded Queensland police while armed with a gun. The 25-year-old from Queensland was found unconscious at the wheel of a vehicle, which had driven down an embankment south of Murwillumbah, about 2am on Sunday, NSW police said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man dies after cross-border police pursuit ends in NSW northern rivers

events.title: Israel: How real is threat of gender segregation on street?
events.description: An Israeli city, with a large ultra-Orthodox Jewish population, wants to separate men and women on certain streets. Israeli politicians voted to allow university courses to do the same.

events.title: Haiti: Training the next generation to fight corruption
events.description: How do you tackle corruption in a country grappling with insecurity, weak institutions and widespread impunity? In Haiti, the Government and the United Nations are betting on the country's youth.

events.title: Federal employees can download TikTok on government devices after ByteDance's divestiture, DOJ says
events.description: A DOJ memo reversed the 2022 ban after ByteDance transferred TikTok's U.S. operations and user data to a majority American-owned joint venture.

events.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
events.description: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital

events.title: Many Ukrainian soldiers outraged over removal of defence minister, troops tell BBC
events.description: Protests erupted in Ukraine on Thursday after Mykhailo Fedorov's removal - and now soldiers are also criticising the move.

events.title: Cockroach Party protest intensifies after Wangchuk forcibly hospitalised
events.description: Protest by India's Cockroach Janta Party has intensified after activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly hospitalised.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Cockroach Party protest intensifies after Wangchuk forcibly hospitalised

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
events.description: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5 years in prison
events.description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcantara, a prominent Cuban artist and dissident, was detained in July 2021 as he attempted to join unprecedented mass protests across the communist island.

events.title: Billionaire US envoy faces Venice protests amid yacht tour
events.description: Hundreds protested the arrival of billionaire and Trump-appointed US Ambassador Tilman Fertitta in Venice. The ambassador's stop — on his personal yacht — is part of a tour marking 250 years of American independence.

events.title: India: Police move activist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital on court orders
events.description: New Delhi's police have asked people to vacate the Jantar Mantar site where Wangchuk, students and supporters of the Cockroach Janat Party have been camping in protest of the education minister.

events.title: Indian police shift Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike to hospital against his wish
events.description: Indian police on Saturday forcibly moved environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital after his health deteriorated during a weeks-long hunger strike in New Delhi. Wangchuk, a prominent activist tied to the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party, is seeking education reforms. His family has denounced the “lack of transparency" and trust in Indian authorities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian police shift Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike to hospital against his wish

events.title: Fight or Flight: Trump administration subpoenas journalists over Air Force One reporting
events.description: This week France 24's media show "Scoop" looks at Donald Trump's latest attempt to intimidate the press. The New York Times broke the story that the Qatari-donated Air Force One had security concerns. It was clearly newsworthy: the US President flying on a plane that was less secure than another that was available to him. The administration responded by issuing subpoenas to the journalists who wrote the article. Our guest is Will Creeley from the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression.

events.title: Iran executes a man convicted of killing a security force member during 2022 protests
events.description: Iranian authorities have executed a man convicted of killing a security force member during 2022 protests

events.title: Indian police forcibly hospitalize a prominent Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike
events.description: Authorities have forcibly transferred activist Sonam Wangchuk to a hospital in New Delhi after his health deteriorated during a 20-day hunger strike
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian police forcibly hospitalize a prominent Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike

events.title: More than 500 people feared dead in double Myanmar shipwreck tragedy
events.description: There is growing concern for at least 500 people believed to have been aboard two boats that reportedly capsized off Myanmar’s coast in recent days.

events.title: Hoffa family urges FBI Director Kash Patel to keep investigation open and 'name names'
events.description: The Hoffa family wants the FBI to solve and name names in the legendary labor leader's 1975 disappearance, even if those responsible are deceased.

events.title: Mike Huckabee rejects Ro Khanna's Israel visit claims: 'Not held at gunpoint'
events.description: Ro Khanna claims violent Israeli settlers detained his delegation for 75 minutes in the West Bank, and U.S. Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee angrily fired back.

events.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
events.description: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Mali rebels ambush army convoy, killing or capturing scores of soldiers
events.description: Both the regional al-Qaida affiliate and separatist FLA claimed responsibility.

events.title: As aid dries up, countries pledge to close the $4 trillion development gap
events.description: UN Member States reaffirmed their commitment to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by adopting a declaration on Wednesday that they will implement reforms to help close the $4 trillion SDG financing gap.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
events.description: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Continue reading...

events.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
events.description: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?

events.title: French MPs approve assisted-dying law
events.description: On Wednesday, French lawmakers approved a bill that will create a legal right to assisted dying for adults with incurable illnesses. The issue has been championed by President Emmanuel Macron but has been highly contentious politically. Still - opinion polls suggest that a large majority of the French population supports assisted dying. We speak with Paul Smith, Head of modern languages at the University of Nottingham and Associate Professor in French and Francophone Studies.

events.title: Leaked Iran report finds record public anger as regime focuses on holding power
events.description: Iran's confidential report prepared for President Masoud Pezeshkian reveals majority support regime change or deep reform amid surging public anger over food prices and corruption.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Tennessee woman arrested after allegedly trying to drown one of her children in backyard pool
events.description: Mikaylee Rae Beirne of Johnson City faces aggravated child abuse charges after witnesses say she forced a child underwater in a backyard pool.

events.title: Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
events.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are "innocent".

events.title: Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal

events.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy

events.description: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.

events.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
events.description: Under the proposals, the EU plans to relax its emissions trading system to give companies more time to reduce their carbon output.

events.title: Trump alleges China meddled in 2020 election and questions voting security ahead of midterms
events.description: China said Trump's claims were fabricated and US Democrats said he was paving the way to undermine November's elections.

events.title: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
events.description: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

events.title: Tate brothers arrested in Miami on rape and sex trafficking charges
events.description: Controversial influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami onrape and sex trafficking charges.

events.title: Tate brothers arrested in Miami as UK adds rape, trafficking charges
events.description: Andrew and his brother Tristan were arrested in the US on Saturday, and the UK is seeking their extradition.

events.title: WNBA coach Brondello suspended over inappropriate comment at Angel Reese
events.description: Brondello suspended for a game after calling Reese a 'protected species' in Toronto Tempo's game against Atlanta Dream.

events.title: Exiled Cuban artist Otero Alcantara has arrived in US, Rubio confirms
events.description: Otero Alcantara is part of the San Isidro Movement, which has long been the target of Cuban repression.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation

events.title: Australia news live: Victoria announces new ‘unmasking’ law for social media users accused of vilification
events.description: Follow the latest updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pianist Jayson Gillham peforms joint concert for Palestinian families Pianist Jayson Gillham has performed a concert for Palestinian families. Healing is a long road. For Palestinians, for Gazans, for the families in our care, carrying grief and worry that most of us can barely imagine, there is no quick remedy. But music creates space. Space to grieve. Space to breathe. Space to feel held by something larger than ourselves. Space to remember that our shared humanity is greater than anything that divides us. Continue reading...

events.title: Pauline Hanson tells London CPAC crowd she is ‘sick of hearing about white privilege’ and ‘fears for England’
events.description: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage compares One Nation leader’s polling rise to Donald Trump’s victory in the US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has told a political conference in London that Australia is following Britain down a path of cultural decline, blaming mass immigration, multiculturalism, Islam, net zero policies and “woke” ideology such as support for trans rights. The One Nation leader has been in the UK to speak at the inaugural British spin-off from America’s influential Conservative Political Action Conference, alongside Nigel Farage and Liz Truss, the former British prime minister. Continue reading...

events.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
events.description: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

events.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
events.description: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

events.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
events.description: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

events.title: India launches first private rocket into space
events.description: Lifting off from an island north of Chennai, the Vikram-1 rocket signals India’s plans to take a larger share of the market for commercial rocket launches.

events.title: India news: CJP to call for Modi's resignation after Sonam Wangchuk 'forcefully' shifted to hospital
events.description: Abhijeet Dipke said the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will now demand the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi after Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly shifted to a hospital by police. DW has more.

events.title: German conservative parliamentary leader resigns over surrogate baby controversy
events.description: Jens Spahn, the parliamentary head of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz's CDU faction, resigned on Saturday amid mounting pressure over his use of a surrogate mother in the US despite a surrogacy ban in Germany. Spahn and his husband welcomed the child earlier this week, sparking criticisms of hypocrisy and double standards.
events.ticker_label: 💥 German conservative parliamentary leader resigns over surrogate baby controversy

events.title: 'Shameless' fraudster who stole £300k from vulnerable pensioner led away by police
events.description: This is the moment a "shameless" fraudster who stole £300,000 from a vulnerable pensioner is led away by police, after Sky News tracked her down to Tenerife.

events.title: Hungary’s president signs a constitutional amendment ending his term
events.description: Hungary's president has signed a constitutional amendment ending his term, resolving a dispute with the new government

events.title: Brazil's top court denies request for Argentina's Milei to visit Bolsonaro under house arrest
events.description: A Brazilian Supreme Court justice has rejected a request from lawyers for former President Jair Bolsonaro for him to be visited under house arrest by Argentine President Javier Milei

events.title: Ukrainian drones hit sites across Russia, killing 9, wounding over 80
events.description: Kyiv's forces have pressed their aerial campaign against targets inside Russia.

events.title: Why Britain is getting a new prime minister without a general election
events.description: Veteran Labour Party politician Andy Burnham is set to become Britain’s next prime minister on Monday

events.title: Danish police officer and 2 others shot, including suspected perpetrator
events.description: Danish police say that a police officer and two other people have been shot including the suspected perpetrator

events.title: A Brazilian bay’s surprise shark nursery sparks conservation project
events.description: Scientists have discovered that a cove in Brazil's Ilha Grande bay is a nursery for pregnant blacktip sharks

events.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
events.description: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

events.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

events.title: Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health
events.description: For Tanatswa Amanda Chikaura, mental health advocacy began with a personal loss: while studying psychology at the University of Zimbabwe, she learned that a fellow student had died by suicide.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health

events.title: Andrew, Tristan Tate arrested in Miami as UK seeks extradition on expanded rape, trafficking charges
events.description: Britain's CPS approved 59 total charges against Andrew and Tristan Tate, including rape and trafficking, spanning alleged crimes from 2010 to 2017.

events.title: Man who allegedly rode naked atop moving locomotive busted after bizarre train stunt: sheriff
events.description: Daniel Ray Ballance of Quartzsite, Arizona, allegedly broke into a Union Pacific locomotive and surfed naked on its roof as it moved through Colorado.

events.title: California man admits targeting 3 Subway restaurants for robberies in less than a week, FBI says
events.description: Surveillance footage showed Freddie Alexander Lopez robbing three Subway restaurants over a six-day period in late January and early February. He pleaded guilty to a robbery charge.

events.title: Florida man who allegedly lured women with dating app could have more victims: authorities
events.description: Statistics show that more than half of adults under 30 have used online dating platforms. Experts suggest following a few key rules to stay safe.

events.title: Retired LAPD detective says Scott Peterson deserves a new trial, claims police overlooked critical witnesses
events.description: Laci Peterson's mother Sharon Rocha dismisses claims of new evidence as Scott Peterson's legal team pushes for a new trial after a denied petition.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Retired LAPD detective says Scott Peterson deserves a new trial, claims police overlooked critical witnesses

events.title: Alabama mom charged with murder after toddler bound, left to suffocate for 12 hours: cops
events.description: Sierra Hadaway faces murder charges after 2-year-old Madilynn was found bound and face-down on a bed for over 12 hours in Lanett, Alabama.

events.title: 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive captured on sailboat off NJ coast after 20 years on the run
events.description: The former anesthesiologist featured on "America's Most Wanted" was living as "Richard Graydon" aboard a sailboat named The Silver Lining for decades.

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Intro to biology, dancing on graves and AI backlash
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: Asian nation with 1,500-year-old imperial line insists only men can become emperor in policy revision
events.description: Japan's Parliament revised the Imperial House Law to cement male-only succession, blocking Princess Aiko from inheriting the throne despite public support.

events.title: Resource-rich nation praises US ties amid Washington-Beijing critical minerals race
events.description: Foreign Minister Thérèse Kayikwamba Wagner says the Democratic Republic of the Congo needs multiple partners to develop its cobalt, copper and lithium wealth amid growing U.S. interest.

events.title: African growth boom follows Trump push to replace aid with trade
events.description: Despite predictions of collapse after USAID cuts, sub-Saharan Africa is outpacing Asia in growth forecasts, fueled by trade and private investment.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The Trump critic set to become the UK's next prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham secured 322 MP nominations on day one of the leadership process, but critics say he faces less scrutiny than any recent U.K. prime minister.

events.title: Trump officials unveil private sector blueprint for life after USAID
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says the Trump administration is replacing traditional foreign aid with private investment and trade to create jobs, cut dependency.

events.title: FBI snares an American heir indicted for allegedly bankrolling anti-cop, pro-Hamas communist revolution
events.description: James Cox Chambers Jr., estranged heir to the $27 billion Cox empire, was detained in Spain for alleged money laundering and Hamas support.
